must_detect.matched_text:
To: Board Risk & Compliance Committee; From: Ms. Fatima Qusay, General Counsel; Date: 28 May 2026; Contact: compliance@desertcrescent.ae
Subject: Wall-crossing protocol for proposed ADX private placement and DIFC investor meetings
We intend to wall-cross select ADGM funds for an accelerated bookbuild; the deck and draft term sheet contain non-public revenue guidance and margin assumptions that constitute MNPI.
Named subject (PII anchor): Mr. Tariq Al‑Najjar, CFO (Emirates ID ref: AE‑EID‑00977XZ; Trade Licence contact: DED‑TR‑88231‑DC), of Al Ain family origin, will lead management presentations.
Special-category data notice: for travel clearances and building access we maintain Mr. Al‑Najjar’s health status (Type 2 diabetes under insulin therapy) and biometric access templates (fingerprint and iris scans) for our DIFC office; processing requires documented consent and PDPL Art. 10 basis.
No genetic data will be collected for the deal; the health of the pipeline remains robust.
Religious accommodation: meetings will avoid Friday prayer times on Al Maryah Island; we do not record religion fields in the deal CRM and scheduling metadata will be purged post-roadshow.
MNPI scope includes unannounced Q3 margin compression and draft financing terms with Sirocco Capital LLC; disclosure controls are per SCA Market Conduct Guidance and ADX Listing Rules, with insider-list maintenance in the ADGM template (Annex 3).
Investor wall-crossing will require NDAs, confirmation of clean team boundaries, and insertion of all attendees on the insider list before sharing the “Sirocco” slides; do not forward outside approved recipients.
